body:
## Summary
Fixes the bug where `cartTotal()` in `src/cart.js` returned `NaN` for an empty cart.

The reduce/accumulator was seeded from `items[0]` (undefined on an empty cart). Now `reduce()` is seeded with an initial value of `0`, so an empty cart correctly totals `0` and populated carts continue to sum correctly.

## Testing
- `npm test` passes, including a new regression test asserting `cartTotal([]) === 0`.

Closes #1

Note: PR targets `baseline` because the repository was empty on clone (no `main` content existed); the buggy baseline was pushed to a `baseline` branch to provide a review base.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Fix cart total showing NaN on empty cart
